# Main objective
This is the ruleset and best practices for better experience for users with TDAH and TSA.
The objective is to Reduce cognitive fatigue, anxiety, and improve understanding for neurodivergent users.

# Best Practices

## 1. Consistent structure
Repeat elements in the same order and in the same place on each page.
Use the same words or icons for recurring elements.
## 2. Content clarity
Layout: short sections, one idea per sentence, 3-4 sentences per paragraph.
Headings: clear, descriptive, to facilitate navigation.
Language: simple, without metaphors or idioms.
Icons: explicit, accompanied by visual labels.
Numbers: rounded, avoid complex fractions and percentages.
Links: descriptive text, underlined to be clearly visible.
<!-- ## 3. Reduce distractions
Avoid content that moves, flashes, or plays automatically.
Allow the user to stop or pause these elements.
## 4. Do not rely on memory
Clear instructions at the moment they are needed.
Allow copy-paste of passwords or codes.
Warn the user of necessary information before starting a form.
Pre-fill fields already known.
## 5. Give more time
Remove time limits or allow them to be adjusted.
Allow saving a form to finish later.
Avoid duration estimates: prefer “10 questions” to “5 minutes”. -->
